ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Milo hot drink; closest parent 'chocolate beverage' (FOODON_03301710) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC for chocolate-flavoured drink used as closest parent; no specific Milo entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 'Chocolate drink' used as closest parent; no Milo-specific SNOMED code exists.

preparation_method: dissolved in hot water or hot milk
main_ingredients: Milo powder (malted barley extract, cocoa, sugar, skimmed milk powder, vitamins and minerals)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of hot Milo prepared with approximately 20 g of Milo powder dissolved in 180 ml of hot water or hot milk, as commonly served at Singapore hawker centres and cafes.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Milo Chocolate Malt Beverage Powder (Nestle)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central data for Nestle Milo chocolate malt beverage powder (per 100 g powder basis), cross-referenced with Nestle Singapore Milo product nutrition panel. Values represent the prepared hot drink reconstituted with water at standard dilution (approximately 20 g powder per 200 ml water), recalculated to per-100 g of prepared beverage. Energy, macros, and key micronutrients are scaled accordingly. Where USDA data for the specific Nestle Milo Singapore formulation was not available, the closest Nestle Milo branded entry in USDA Branded Foods was used.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; chocolate-flavoured malted milk drink analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Nestle Milo hot drink prepared with water was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate based on chocolate-flavoured malted milk drinks reported in Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008), International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Milo is a malted barley and cocoa-based beverage with moderate sugar content; GI 40 (low category) is consistent with similar malted chocolate beverages in the peer-reviewed GI literature. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 17.0) / 100 = 6.8, where carbohydrate per 200 g serving = 17.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content in prepared drink
hypertension.guidance: Hot Milo prepared with water contains approximately 60 mg sodium per 200 ml serving, which is low and unlikely to contribute meaningfully to blood pressure elevation in hypertensive individuals when consumed in moderation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and moderate carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hot Milo contains approximately 14 g of sugar per 200 ml serving primarily from added sugars in the Milo powder formulation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and consider unsweetened or reduced-sugar Milo variants to manage postprandial glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and low sodium in prepared drink
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hot Milo prepared with water is low in saturated fat (approximately 0.6 g per 200 ml serving) and sodium, making it a relatively heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed without added condensed milk or full-fat dairy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Milo (美禄热饮) is a popular hot malted chocolate beverage widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and across Southeast Asia. It is prepared by dissolving Nestle Milo powder — a blend of malted barley extract, cocoa, sugar, skimmed milk powder, and added vitamins and minerals — in hot water or hot milk. The resulting drink has a warm, mildly sweet, chocolatey-malty flavour with a smooth, slightly thick consistency. In Singapore, hot Milo is a staple at hawker centres, kopitiams (traditional coffee shops), school canteens, and cafes, and is deeply embedded in local food culture. It is commonly ordered as 'Milo Panas' in Malay-speaking contexts. The drink provides moderate energy (approximately 90 kcal per 200 ml serving when prepared with water), with carbohydrates as the primary macronutrient source. It contains added sugars from the Milo powder formulation, which is relevant for individuals managing blood glucose levels. Milo is fortified with B vitamins, calcium, iron, and phosphorus, contributing to its nutritional profile beyond simple sugar beverages. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low category) based on analogous malted chocolate drink data from peer-reviewed GI literature. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, portion control and awareness of added sugar content are important. The beverage is low in sodium and saturated fat, making it broadly suitable for cardiovascular health when consumed in moderation and without high-fat dairy additions such as condensed milk.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a cup of hot Milo?
What is the GI of Milo?
How much sugar is in hot Milo?
Can I drink Milo if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Nestle Milo branded product data and cross-referenced with Nestle Singapore product nutrition panel, recalculated to per-100 g of prepared beverage at standard dilution. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry for hot Milo prepared drink was available. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature for similar malted chocolate beverages. Confidence is medium due to the adaptation and estimation steps required.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent hot Milo prepared with water at approximately 20 g powder per 200 ml water (10% w/v dilution), recalculated to per-100 g prepared beverage basis. Values will differ if prepared with full-fat or condensed milk. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Nestle Milo product nutrition panel. 5. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 12. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for Nestle Milo powder. 13. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 14. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Milo hot drink prepared with water was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 15. The food is classified as Singaporean cuisine for multilingual naming purposes as Milo is a culturally embedded beverage in Singapore, though the product is manufactured by Nestle and consumed across Southeast Asia. 16. Price data from GrabFood Singapore (SGD 5.95 at Hai Kah Lang, Funan Mall) is preserved as contextual metadata but not used as a nutritional source.

improvement_actions_taken: Classified cuisine as Singaporean to trigger correct multilingual naming requirements (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Added Chinese simplified and traditional local names, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names.
Adapted USDA FoodData Central Nestle Milo branded data to per-100 g prepared beverage basis at standard 10% w/v dilution.
Set GI=40 as analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed GI tables for malted chocolate drinks.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Ensured natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
Used only allowed match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none) for all ontology fields.
